channel.title: REINFORCE on Jubzinas | AI & Blockchain
channel.description: Recent content in REINFORCE on Jubzinas | AI & Blockchain

item.title: Policy Networks: Learning to Act Directly
item.description:
Unlike Q-Learning, which learns the value of actions and then derives a policy, a policy network learns the policy directly. In other words, instead of asking “how good is this action in this state?”, it asks “what action should I take right now?”

That makes policy gradients especially useful when the action space is small but the optimal behavior depends on long-term consequences that are hard to encode with a table of values.
